title: feat: Switch to enum queue mode, add 'replace' mode.
body:
Follow up to https://github.com/Acly/krita-ai-diffusion/pull/1879

Kind of janky because of the fact that job queue tasks are split up before the client- I make clear-queue a property of the first job.

Not sure if the enum is in the right place.

Tentatively planning to implement ctrl-click as "Alternate queue mode".

files_modified.patch:
@@ -37,6 +37,12 @@
 from .resolution import compute_bounds, compute_relative_bounds
 
 
+class QueueMode(Enum):
+    back = 0
+    front = 1
+    replace = 2
+
+
 class Workspace(Enum):
     generation = 0
     upscaling = 1
@@ -95,7 +101,7 @@ class Model(QObject, ObservableProperties):
     seed = Property(0, persist=True)
     fixed_seed = Property(False, persist=True)
     resolution_multiplier = Property(1.0, persist=True)
-    queue_front = Property(False, persist=True)
+    queue_mode = Property(QueueMode.back, persist=True)
     translation_enabled = Property(True, persist=True)
     progress_kind = Property(ProgressKind.generation)
     progress = Property(0.0)
@@ -109,7 +115,7 @@ class Model(QObject, ObservableProperties):
     seed_changed = pyqtSignal(int)
     fixed_seed_changed = pyqtSignal(bool)
     resolution_multiplier_changed = pyqtSignal(float)
-    queue_front_changed = pyqtSignal(bool)
+    queue_mode_changed = pyqtSignal(QueueMode)
     translation_enabled_changed = pyqtSignal(bool)
     progress_kind_changed = pyqtSignal(ProgressKind)
     progress_changed = pyqtSignal(float)
@@ -148,6 +154,14 @@ def _forward_error(self, error: str):
         self.report_error(error if error else no_error)
 
     def generate(self):
+        """Enqueue image generation for the current setup."""
+        self._generate(self.queue_mode)
+
+    def generate_replace(self):
+        """Enqueue image generation with queue mode set to replace."""
+        self._generate(QueueMode.replace)
+
+    def _generate(self, queue_mode: QueueMode):
         """Enqueue image generation for the current setup."""
         ok, msg = self._doc.check_color_mode()
         if not ok and msg:
@@ -160,7 +174,7 @@ def generate(self):
             self.report_error(util.log_error(e))
             return
         self.clear_error()
-        jobs = self.enqueue_jobs(input, JobKind.diffusion, job_params, self.batch_count)
+        jobs = self.enqueue_jobs(input, JobKind.diffusion, job_params, self.batch_count, queue_mode)
         eventloop.run(_report_errors(self, jobs))
 
     def _prepare_workflow(self, dryrun=False):
@@ -240,23 +254,35 @@ def _prepare_workflow(self, dryrun=False):
         return input, job_params
 
     async def enqueue_jobs(
-        self, input: WorkflowInput, kind: JobKind, params: JobParams, count: int = 1
+        self,
+        input: WorkflowInput,
+        kind: JobKind,
+        params: JobParams,
+        count: int = 1,
+        queue_mode: QueueMode | None = None,
     ):
         sampling = ensure(input.sampling)
         params.has_mask = input.images is not None and input.images.hires_mask is not None
+        queue_mode = queue_mode or self.queue_mode
+
+        if queue_mode is QueueMode.replace:
+            await self._connection.client.clear_queue()
+            self.clear_queued()
+            queue_mode = QueueMode.back
 
         for i in range(count):
             next_seed = sampling.seed + i * settings.batch_size
             input = replace(input, sampling=replace(sampling, seed=next_seed))
             params.seed = next_seed
             job = self.jobs.add(kind, copy(params))
-            await self._enqueue_job(job, input)
+            front = queue_mode is QueueMode.front
+            await self._enqueue_job(job, input, front=front)
 
-    async def _enqueue_job(self, job: Job, input: WorkflowInput):
+    async def _enqueue_job(self, job: Job, input: WorkflowInput, front: bool = False):
         if not self.jobs.any_executing():
             self.progress = 0.0
         client = self._connection.client
-        job.id = await client.enqueue(input, self.queue_front)
+        job.id = await client.enqueue(input, front)
 
     def _prepare_upscale_image(self, dryrun=False):
         assert not self.arch.is_edit, "Edit models do not support upscaling"
@@ -479,15 +505,17 @@ def generate_control_layer(self, control: ControlLayer):
         return job
 
     def cancel(self, active=False, queued=False):
-        if queued:
-            to_remove = [job for job in self.jobs if job.state is JobState.queued]
-            if len(to_remove) > 0:
-                self._connection.clear_queue()
-                for job in to_remove:
-                    self.jobs.remove(job)
+        if queued and self.clear_queued():
+            self._connection.clear_queue()
         if active and self.jobs.any_executing():
             self._connection.interrupt()
 
+    def clear_queued(self):
+        to_remove = [job for job in self.jobs if job.state is JobState.queued]
+        for job in to_remove:
+            self.jobs.remove(job)
+        return len(to_remove) > 0
+
     def report_error(self, error: Error | str):
         if isinstance(error, str):
             error = Error.from_string(error, ErrorKind.server_error)

@@ -761,6 +761,7 @@ def model(self, model: Model):
                 self.add_region_button.clicked.connect(model.regions.create_region_group),
                 self.region_prompt.activated.connect(model.generate),
                 self.generate_button.clicked.connect(model.generate),
+                self.generate_button.ctrl_clicked.connect(model.generate_replace),
             ]
             self.region_prompt.regions = model.regions
             self.custom_inpaint.model = model

@@ -43,7 +43,16 @@
 from ..client import filter_supported_styles, resolve_arch
 from ..properties import Binding, Bind, bind, bind_combo
 from ..jobs import JobState, JobKind
-from ..model import Model, Workspace, SamplingQuality, ProgressKind, ErrorKind, Error, no_error
+from ..model import (
+    Model,
+    Workspace,
+    SamplingQuality,
+    ProgressKind,
+    ErrorKind,
+    Error,
+    no_error,
+    QueueMode,
+)
 from ..text import edit_attention, select_on_cursor_pos
 from ..localization import translate as _
 from ..util import ensure
@@ -131,11 +140,12 @@ def __init__(self, supports_batch=True, parent: QWidget | None = None):
         self._layout.addLayout(resolution_multiplier_layout, 2, 1)
 
         enqueue_label = QLabel(_("Enqueue"), self)
-        self._queue_front_combo = QComboBox(self)
-        self._queue_front_combo.addItem(_("in Front (new jobs first)"), True)
-        self._queue_front_combo.addItem(_("at the Back"), False)
+        self._queue_mode_combo = QComboBox(self)
+        self._queue_mode_combo.addItem(_("at the Back"), QueueMode.back)
+        self._queue_mode_combo.addItem(_("in Front (new jobs first)"), QueueMode.front)
+        self._queue_mode_combo.addItem(_("Replace Queue"), QueueMode.replace)
         self._layout.addWidget(enqueue_label, 3, 0)
-        self._layout.addWidget(self._queue_front_combo, 3, 1)
+        self._layout.addWidget(self._queue_mode_combo, 3, 1)
 
         cancel_label = QLabel(_("Cancel"), self)
         self._layout.addWidget(cancel_label, 4, 0)
@@ -171,7 +181,7 @@ def model(self, model: Model):
             self._model.fixed_seed_changed.connect(self._randomize_seed.setEnabled),
             self._randomize_seed.clicked.connect(self._model.generate_seed),
             model.resolution_multiplier_changed.connect(self._update_resolution_multiplier),
-            bind_combo(self._model, "queue_front", self._queue_front_combo),
+            bind_combo(self._model, "queue_mode", self._queue_mode_combo),
             model.jobs.count_changed.connect(self._update_cancel_buttons),
         ]
 
@@ -737,6 +747,8 @@ def _create_action(self, name: str, workspace: Workspace):
 
 
 class GenerateButton(QPushButton):
+    ctrl_clicked = pyqtSignal()
+
     def __init__(self, kind: JobKind, parent: QWidget):
         super().__init__(parent)
         self.model = root.active_model
@@ -769,6 +781,18 @@ def enterEvent(self, a0: QEvent | None):
     def leaveEvent(self, a0: QEvent | None):
         self._cost = 0
 
+    def mouseReleaseEvent(self, e: QMouseEvent | None):
+        if (
+            e
+            and e.button() == Qt.MouseButton.LeftButton
+            and bool(e.modifiers() & Qt.KeyboardModifier.ControlModifier)
+        ):
+            self.ctrl_clicked.emit()
+            e.accept()
+            self.setDown(False)
+        else:
+            super().mouseReleaseEvent(e)
+
     def paintEvent(self, a0: QPaintEvent | None) -> None:
         opt = QStyleOption()
         opt.initFrom(self)

steps.operation: Introduce an enum representing queue insertion behavior (back/front/replace)
steps.reason: Replace a boolean (queue_front) with a scalable representation that can add additional modes (e.g., replace) without further schema churn.
steps.side_effects: Any persisted settings, bindings, or code paths expecting a bool for queueing will need migration/updates to handle an Enum value.

steps.operation: Replace the persisted model property/signal from boolean queue_front to enum queue_mode
steps.reason: Store and broadcast queue behavior as an enum so UI and enqueue logic can select among multiple modes consistently.
steps.side_effects: Property persistence format changes; existing user settings may default/reset unless there is implicit enum serialization compatibility in Property().

steps.operation: Refactor generation entrypoint to delegate to a private generator that accepts a queue mode
steps.reason: Centralize queue-mode handling so the default generate path uses the configured queue_mode while allowing alternate generation triggers to override it.
steps.side_effects: Any callers relying on Model.generate’s prior inline behavior now go through a wrapper, which could affect debugging/stack traces but not semantics.

steps.operation: Add an alternate generation method that forces replace mode
steps.reason: Expose a dedicated action for UI/shortcuts (e.g., ctrl-click) to enqueue with replace semantics without changing the persisted queue_mode selection.
steps.side_effects: Adds a new public API method that other UI components might start calling; must remain stable for signal connections.

steps.operation: Implement the private generation helper and plumb queue_mode into enqueue_jobs
steps.reason: Allow per-request queue mode (default/back/front/replace) to influence how jobs are enqueued, while keeping the core workflow preparation unchanged.
steps.side_effects: Changes the signature/usage of enqueue_jobs from internal callers; any other internal call sites must be updated to pass (or rely on default) queue_mode.

steps.operation: Extend enqueue_jobs to support replace semantics by clearing server/client queues and then enqueueing at back
steps.reason: Implement 'replace' mode despite the queue being split into multiple tasks by ensuring the first job triggers a queue clear before new jobs are added.
steps.side_effects: Clearing the remote queue may cancel unrelated queued jobs from other actions; local job list is also mutated (clear_queued), which can affect UI counters and cancel state.

steps.operation: Update job enqueue helper to accept a computed 'front' boolean and pass it to the client enqueue call
steps.reason: Decouple enqueue position from model state (previously self.queue_front) so per-call queue_mode can control placement deterministically.
steps.side_effects: Any direct callers of _enqueue_job must now pass the appropriate front flag or rely on the default; enqueue ordering may change if mismatched.

steps.operation: Refactor queued-cancel path to reuse a shared local queued-job clearing routine
steps.reason: Avoid duplicated logic and ensure consistent behavior between explicit cancel-queued and replace-queue operations by using a single queued-removal implementation.
steps.side_effects: If clear_queued() semantics differ subtly from the previous inline logic, the conditions under which _connection.clear_queue() is invoked may change.

steps.operation: Add a helper that removes all locally tracked queued jobs and reports whether any were removed
steps.reason: Provide a reusable primitive for both cancel(queued=True) and replace-mode enqueue to keep local job state consistent with server-side queue clearing.
steps.side_effects: UI elements observing jobs collection changes will update more often; any logic depending on queued jobs remaining for audit/history may be affected.

steps.operation: Wire ctrl-click on the generate button to call model.generate_replace
steps.reason: Expose the new replace mode through an ergonomic gesture without changing the user’s selected default queue mode.
steps.side_effects: Ctrl-click behavior may conflict with other modifier-based interactions; users may accidentally clear their queue if they ctrl-click unintentionally.

steps.operation: Update the UI to use an enum-backed queue mode combo and add a ctrl-click-capable GenerateButton signal/emission
steps.reason: Present all queue modes (back/front/replace) in the enqueue selector and emit a dedicated signal when the user ctrl-clicks, enabling alternate queue mode behavior.
steps.side_effects: Combo binding now persists an enum rather than a bool; overriding mouseReleaseEvent changes click handling and could impact accessibility, press/release visual state, and other mouse interactions.

step_plan.summary: This PR replaces the boolean “queue_front” setting with an enum-based “queue_mode” and adds a new “replace” mode that clears the queue before enqueueing new jobs. It also adds ctrl-click handling on the Generate button to trigger replace-mode generation from the UI.
